items.title: Top-k retrieval
items.scenario: Score 50,000 vector-store chunks against a query and keep the 10 best.
items.explanation: Maintain a *min-heap of size 10*: when scoring a new chunk, push it; if size > 10, pop the smallest. Final heap is the top-10. O(n log k) instead of O(n log n).

items.title: Beam search decoding
items.scenario: Generate text by expanding the top-k most likely partial hypotheses at each step.
items.explanation: A max-heap of (score, hypothesis) tuples keeps the best k candidates per step; you pop the worst when the heap grows past k.

items.title: A* / Dijkstra over a knowledge graph
items.scenario: Find the shortest path between two entities in a graph weighted by edge cost.
items.explanation: A priority queue of (estimated_total_cost, node) lets you expand the most promising node next, not the oldest.

items.title: k-way merge of ranked results
items.scenario: Three retrievers each return sorted lists of (score, chunk); you want one merged sorted stream.
items.explanation: Push the head of each list into a heap; pop the best, replace from its source list. Total work is O(N log k).

items.title: Premium-first request scheduler
items.scenario: Free and paid traffic share one model endpoint; paid users must be served first.
items.explanation: Push `(priority, request)` into a heap; dequeue always returns the highest-priority pending request.
